mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. A force is required to keep an object in motion.
B. An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by a net external force.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law?
options: A. A ball rolling down a hill
B. A car making a turn
C. A book resting on a table
D. A rocket launching into space

mcqs.question: In the absence of external forces, a moving object will...
options: A. Slow down
B. Change direction
C. Stop
D. Continue moving at a constant velocity

mcqs.question: What term describes the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion?
options: A. Inertia
B. Force
C. Mass
D. Acceleration

mcqs.question: If no net force acts on an object, it will...
options: A. Change its speed
B. Change its direction
C. Remain at rest or continue to move uniformly
D. Accelerate

fill_in_the_blanks.question: Newton's First Law is also known as the law of ________.
An object will remain at rest unless acted upon by a ________ force.
The property of an object that resists changes in its motion is called ________.
According to Newton's First Law, an object in motion will continue in ________ unless acted upon by a net external force.
The First Law of Motion was formulated by ________.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in daily life.
What happens to a hockey puck sliding on ice?
What is a net external force?
